item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of all, it isn't destroyed. The foundation contains and studies it, just like it does with everything else.

Also, what about the fact that most victims die within a week? Sure, it cures them, but then they die pretty quickly. Depending on their disease, people might live longer without the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like the message here. This thing isn't an invincibility drug, it's a panacea. If you're going to destroy it because it's immoral, you might as well just start slaughtering doctors, because this is making people better, not forcing them to stay alive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed "It felt like it'd forever when George opened his eyes" to "It felt like it'd been forever since George opened his eyes." to correct grammar. It seemed that either two different ideas got mixed together or a word had been left out. I tried to fix it to the best of my ability without losing what seemed to be the general idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, i like it. I'm getting the feeling SCP-2333-1 somehow had the proteins work and hes immortal now, but deep down he wants to die.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quick medical note - while MRIs are technically safe to use with most kinds of retained bullets, I doubt the Foundation would risk even a tiny chance of twisting that thing around in his brain.

Perhaps a CT (or CAT if you prefer) scan is more appropriate?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Infection also suppresses of the pain receptors.

I'm thinking this is a typo.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: I'm planning on reworking some of this over the next month or two. I'm posting here to let everyone know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found myself meh-ing until the final interview log. Seriously, Tuck Everlasting was a positively horrifying book for me. This struck the exact same chord. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The reason I said that is because I knew it wasn't going to win, so I was just hoping for the specific slot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Though it would be pretty cool if the Foundation somehow found a way of analyzing inelegance in order to locate anomalous activity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What? I didn't misspell anything. NOOOO…
Thanks for pointing that out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you might mean "intelligence"? "Intelegence" doesn't seem to be a word…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Intelegence"

Thanks for pointing out the typo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is an "inelegance check"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't quite explain why, but I'm just not grabbed by this one. It's written well, and it seems to be a full realization of what you're going for, but I just find myself unmoved. Too good to downvote, but I'm in solid neutral territory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
tbh, I just wanted an early spot in series 3

For some reason this sentiment isn't sitting very well with me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it alright, especially the ending, but overall it's a novote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. I'm not sure if I'll vote for it during the contest, but, if not, I will definitely vote for it after.
It seems like all of the creepy and scare is very, very subtle and only works if the reader tries to empathize with the victim. If I read this correctly, its a serum that only worked once, but when it does work it makes you an immortal superhuman who is at complete peace with, and near-eagerly awaiting their own death. If I read that correctly, it's a perversely bad situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I can only speak for myself, but I really like this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I approached the contest as "fiction about science", but tbh, I just wanted an early spot in series 3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this would be a fine mainlister, as a 2000 entry it doesn't seem to hit the nail. I mean I think it certainly qualifies as science fiction, this kind of thing is exactly WHY there is science fiction, but it just doesn't seem to carry the banner for the theme. At any rate, I'll upvote this where ever it goes on the list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed.
And as a note, only his last name was redacted.

EDIT: Oh I see it. NOW it's fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is not badly written, but I don't find it interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not an overly amazing entry, but it's a solid skip. I like the life extension angle and that final note. The last interview is good, too.

You've got a 'has has' in there, a 'thinks' instead of 'things', and you redact -1's name in the description, but not the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty ordinary. Nothing too special. Just glad that the researchers didn't make zombies yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, this doesn't look very anomalous to me. Somewhat futuristic medical technology, but nothing that really made me sit up and say "okay that's weird". If I saw a real-life news story about this, my reaction wouldn't be "the laws of physics were broken" or even "wow we are really living in the future", it would be "that was impressive but dangerous work, it's a shame it doesn't have wider real world applications".

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Here is where the picture is from:
http://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Paper_Micrograph_Bright.png
This is a rewrite of my old entry for this contest. It uses the same approach of "Fiction about Science".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview really made me, I feel sorry for 2000-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: so wait, can these people die through other means or are hey immortal for the week?
